macro.intro: The macroeconomic environment is characterized by rising inflation with CPI YoY at 330.29, up from 327.46. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield remains stable at 1.9%, indicating moderate real returns. The Fed Funds Rate is unchanged at 3.64%, reflecting a cautious monetary stance. No major economic events are scheduled for today, allowing markets to focus on geopolitical developments.

snapshot.note: Eyes fourth weekly gain on US-Iran peace hopes

snapshot.note: Falls on prospects for Iran war talks

setups.name: Gold Long on Geopolitical Easing
setups.rationale: Gold is holding steady at $1985.00, supported by hopes for a US-Iran peace deal. The CPI YoY inflation at 330.29 suggests continued demand for inflation hedges. The absence of major economic events today allows focus on geopolitical developments.

setups.name: WTI Short on Peace Prospects
setups.rationale: WTI Crude Oil is trading at $72.50, down 1.20% today as prospects for talks to end the Iran war weigh on prices. The 10Y-2Y Yield Spread at 0.54% suggests moderate economic growth expectations.

setups.name: S&P 500 Long on Positive Sentiment
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is trading at 4350.00, up 0.30% today, driven by positive sentiment amid easing geopolitical tensions. The unemployment rate at 4.3% indicates a strong labor market, supporting equities.

contrarian.body: The EUR/USD is trading at 1.0950, with the dollar set for a second weekly drop. However, the lack of major economic events today could lead to a technical pullback. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield at 1.9% suggests moderate USD strength potential.
contrarian.warning: A sudden shift in geopolitical sentiment could strengthen the euro. Monitor news closely for any unexpected developments.
